devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । मनुर्विवस्वतः पुत्रः श्राद्धदेव इति श्रुतः । सप्तमो वर्तमानो यस्तदपत्यानि मे शृणु ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca manurvivasvataḥ putraḥ śrāddhadeva iti śrutaḥ saptamo vartamāno yastadapatyāni me śṛṇu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka resumed:
The present, seventh Manu is known as Śrāddhadeva. He is the son of Vivasvān. Now hear from me the names of his sons.

devanagari: इक्ष्वाकुर्नभगश्चैव धृष्टः शर्यातिरेव च । नरिष्यन्तोऽथ नाभागः सप्तमो दिष्ट उच्यते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ikṣvākurnabhagaścaiva dhṛṣṭaḥ śaryātireva ca nariṣyanto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nṝ class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṝ class 9 verb]

translation: They are Ikṣvāku, Nabhaga, Dhṛṣṭa, Śaryāti, Nariṣyanta, Nābhāga and the seventh is called Diṣṭa.

devanagari: तरूषश्च पृषध्रश्च दशमो वसुमान्स्मृतः । मनोर्वैवस्वतस्यैते दशपुत्राः परन्तप ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tarūṣaśca pṛṣadhraśca daśamo vasumānsmṛtaḥ manorvaivasvatasyaite daśaputrāḥ parantapa
translation: Karuṣa, Paruṣa and the tenth is known as Vasumān—these are the ten sons of Vaivasvata Manu, Oh tormentor of the enemies.

devanagari: आदित्या वसवो रुद्रा विश्वेदेवा मरुद्गणाः । अश्विनावृभवो राजन्निन्द्रस्तेषां पुरन्दरः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ādityā vasavo rudrā viśvedevā marudgaṇāḥ aśvināvṛbhavo rājannindrasteṣāṃ purandaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: The (twelve) Adityas, the (eight) Vasus, the (eleven) Rudras. the (ten) Viśvedevas, the (forty-nine) Maruts (wind-god), two Aśvinīkumāras, the (three) Ṛbhus are (the seven grades of) gods and their Indra (ruling god) is Purandara, Oh King.

devanagari: कश्यपोऽत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्च विश्वामित्रोऽथ गौतमः । जमदग्निर्भरद्वाज इति सप्तर्षयः स्मृताः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kaśyapo jamadagnirbharadvāja iti saptarṣayaḥ smṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Kaśyapa, Atri, Vasiṣṭha, Viśvāmitra, Gautama, Jamadagni and Bharadvāja—These seven are regarded as the seven (principal) sages.

devanagari: अत्रापि भगवज्जन्म कश्यपाददितेरभूत् । आदित्यानामवरजो विष्णुर्वामनरूपधृक् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: atrāpi bhagavajjanma kaśyapādaditerabhūt ādityānāmavarajo viṣṇurvāmanarūpadhṛk
translation: Even in this Manvantara, there was the incarnation of the Lord, born of Kaśyapa and Aditi. Lord Viṣṇu, assuming the form of a dwarf, was born as the youngest son of Aditi.

devanagari: सङ्क्षेपतो मयोक्तानि सप्तमन्वन्तराणि ते । भविष्याण्यथ वक्ष्यामि विष्णोः शक्त्यान्वितानि च ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅkṣepato mayoktāni saptamanvantarāṇi te bhaviṣyāṇyatha vakṣyāmi viṣṇoḥ śaktyānvitāni ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ vac class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: The (first) seven Manvantaras have been described to you briefly by me. Now I shall narrate to you the future ones, associated with the powers (incarnations) of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: विवस्वतश्च द्वे जाये विश्वकर्मसुते उभे । संज्ञा छाया च राजेन्द्र ये प्रागभिहिते तव ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vivasvataśca dve jāye viśvakarmasute ubhe saṃjñā chāyā ca rājendra ye prāgabhihite tava
translation: Vivasvān had two wives, both being the daughters of Viśvakarman. They were Sandhyā and Chāyā about whom I have spoken to you, Oh king of kings.[1]

devanagari: तृतीयां वडवामेके तासां संज्ञासुतास्त्रयः । यमो यमी श्राद्धदेवश्छायायाश्च सुताञ्छृणु ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛtīyāṃ vaḍavāmeke tāsāṃ saṃjñāsutāstrayaḥ yamo yamī śrāddhadevaśchāyāyāśca sutāñchṛṇu
translation: Some say that he had a third wife called Vaḍavā.[2] Out of the three wives, the children of Saṃjñā were three—Yama, Yamī and Śrāddhadeva. Now listen to the names of Chāyā’s children—
purport:
Footnote [2]:
But according to the Bhāgavata Purāṇa Supra 6.6.40 Saṃjñā assumed the form of Veḍavā [Vaḍavā?] and gave birth to Aśvinī kumāras.

devanagari: सावर्णिस्तपती कन्या भार्या संवरणस्य या । शनैश्चरस्तृतीयोऽभूदश्विनौ वडवात्मजौ ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sāvarṇistapatī kanyā bhāryā saṃvaraṇasya yā śanaiścarastṛtīyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tap class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tap class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tap class 1 verb]

translation: Sāvarṇi, the daughter Tapatī who became the wife of Sarhvaraṇa and the third son Śanaiścara (the presiding deity of the planet Saturn). The two Aśvinīkumāras were the sons of Vaḍavā.

devanagari: अष्टमेऽन्तर आयाते सावर्णिर्भविता मनुः । निर्मोकविरजस्काद्याः सावर्णितनया नृप ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭame nirmokavirajaskādyāḥ sāvarṇitanayā nṛpa
translation: When the eighth Manvantara will set in, Sāvarṇi will be the Manu. Nirmoka, Virajaska and others are the sons of Sāvarṇi, Oh King.

devanagari: तत्र देवाः सुतपसो विरजा अमृतप्रभाः । तेषां विरोचनसुतो बलिरिन्द्रो भविष्यति ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra devāḥ sutapaso virajā amṛtaprabhāḥ teṣāṃ virocanasuto balirindro bhaviṣyati
translation: Then (in the 8th Manvantara), Sutapas, Virajas and Amṛtaprabhas are going to be the gods. Their ruling god (Indra) will be Bali, the son of Virocana.

devanagari: दत्त्वेमां याचमानाय विष्णवे यः पदत्रयम् । राद्धमिन्द्र पदं हित्वा ततः सिद्धिमवाप्स्यति ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dattvemāṃ yācamānāya viṣṇave yaḥ padatrayam rāddhamindra padaṃ hitvā tataḥ siddhimavāpsyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pad class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ rādh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ rādh class 5 verb]
[nominative single from √ rādh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ rādh class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ rādh class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ rādh class 5 verb]

translation: Having donated the whole of this earth to Viṣṇu who begged for only three paces ofland(in this Manvantara), he will relinquish his eminent position of Indra (attained by him through Viṣṇu’s grace in the next Manvantara), and will attain to final beatitude.

devanagari: योऽसौ भगवता बद्धः प्रीतेन सुतले पुनः । निवेशितोऽधिके स्वर्गादधुनास्ते स्वराडिव ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yo niveśito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[instrumental single from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: That same Bali who was bound down by the Lord who, when pleased, installed him in the Nether world Sutala which is superior to the Svarga in splendour—still rules there like the king of the heavenly regions.

devanagari: गालवो दीप्तिमान्रामो द्रोणपुत्रः कृपस्तथा । ऋष्यशृङ्गः पितास्माकं भगवान्बादरायणः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gālavo dīptimānrāmo droṇaputraḥ kṛpastathā ṛṣyaśṛṅgaḥ pitāsmākaṃ bhagavānbādarāyaṇaḥ
translation: Gālava, the glorious (Paraśu-) Rāma, Aśvatthāman, the son of Droṇa, Kṛpa, Ṛṣyaśṛṅga and our father the venerable Bādarāyaṇa—these will be the seven sages. Even now they continue to occupy their own hermitages through their yogic powers.

devanagari: इमे सप्तर्षयस्तत्र भविष्यन्ति स्वयोगतः । इदानीमासते राजन्स्वे स्व आश्रममण्डले ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ime saptarṣayastatra bhaviṣyanti svayogataḥ idānīmāsate rājansve sva āśramamaṇḍale

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Gālava, the glorious (Paraśu-) Rāma, Aśvatthāman, the son of Droṇa, Kṛpa, Ṛṣyaśṛṅga and our father the venerable Bādarāyaṇa—these will be the seven sages. Even now they continue to occupy their own hermitages through their yogic powers.

devanagari: देवगुह्यात्सरस्वत्यां सार्वभौम इति प्रभुः । स्थानं पुरन्दराद्धृत्वा बलये दास्यतीश्वरः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: devaguhyātsarasvatyāṃ sārvabhauma iti prabhuḥ sthānaṃ purandarāddhṛtvā balaye dāsyatīśvaraḥ
translation: The Lord will incarnate with the name Sārva- bhauma, from Devaguhya and Sarasvatī (as his parents). The powerful Lord will wrest Indrahood from Purandara and confer it on Bali.

devanagari: नवमो दक्षसावर्णिर्मनुर्वरुणसम्भवः । भूतकेतुर्दीप्तकेतुरित्याद्यास्तत्सुता नृप ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: navamo dakṣasāvarṇirmanurvaruṇasambhavaḥ bhūtaketurdīptaketurityādyāstatsutā nṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: The ninth Manu will be Dakṣa-Sāvarṇi who will be born of Varuṇa. Bhūtaketu, Dīptiketu and others[3] will be his sons, Oh King.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.22 adds Dhṛtaketu. Nirāmaya, Pañcahasta and Pṛthuśrava to this list.

devanagari: पारा मरीचिगर्भाद्या देवा इन्द्रो ऽद्भुतः स्मृतः । द्युतिमत्प्रमुखास्तत्र भविष्यन्त्यृषयस्ततः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: pārā marīcigarbhādyā devā indro dyutimatpramukhāstatra bhaviṣyantyṛṣayastataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: Pāras and Marīcigarbhas and others (viz. Sudharmans)[4] will be (the three categories of) gods then. It is traditionally reported that Adbhuta will be the Indra. Then there will be the seven sages of whom Dyutimat[5] is prominent.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.20-21 adds this third category and each category will consist of 12 gods.

Footnote [5]:
The Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.22 gives the complete list as follows:—Dyutimat. Savana. Bhavya, Vasu, Medhātithi, Jyotiṣmān and Satya.

devanagari: आयुष्मतोऽम्बुधारायामृषभो भगवत्कला । भविता येन संराद्धां त्रिलोकीं भोक्ष्यतेऽद्भुतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: āyuṣmato bhavitā yena saṃrāddhāṃ trilokīṃ bhokṣyate
translation: A ray of the Lord will incarnate under the name Ṛṣabha from Āyuṣmat and Ambudharā (as his father and mother). Adbhuta, the Indra, will enjoy the three worlds conquered (for him) by Ṛṣabha.

devanagari: दशमो ब्रह्मसावर्णिरुपश्लोकसुतो मनुः । तत्सुता भूरिषेणाद्या हविष्मत्प्रमुखा द्विजाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: daśamo brahmasāvarṇirupaślokasuto manuḥ tatsutā bhūriṣeṇādyā haviṣmatpramukhā dvijāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: The tenth Manu will be Brahmasāvarṇi, the great son of Upaśloka. His sons are Bhūriṣeṇa and others while Haviṣmat and others will be prominent Brāhmaṇa (sages).

devanagari: हविष्मान्सुकृतः सत्यो जयो मूर्तिस्तदा द्विजाः । सुवासनविरुद्धाद्या देवाः शम्भुः सुरेश्वरः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: haviṣmānsukṛtaḥ satyo jayo mūrtistadā dvijāḥ suvāsanaviruddhādyā devāḥ śambhuḥ sureśvaraḥ
translation: Haviṣmān, Sukṛti, Satya, Jaya, Mūrti will be(some of) the Brāhmaṇa (sages) then. Suvāsana and Viruddha are the gods, and Śambhu is the Lord of gods.

devanagari: विष्वक्सेनो विषूच्यां तु शम्भोः सख्यं करिष्यति । जातः स्वांशेन भगवान्गृहे विश्वसृजो विभुः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣvakseno viṣūcyāṃ tu śambhoḥ sakhyaṃ kariṣyati jātaḥ svāṃśena bhagavāngṛhe viśvasṛjo vibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[locative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[locative single from √ kṛ class 3 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 6 verb]
[locative single from √ kṛ class 3 verb], [locative single from √ kṛ class 6 verb]
[locative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [locative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb]
[locative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [locative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: The Lord, with a part of his Self, will be born in the house of Viśvasṛj (Prajāpati) through Viṣūci, and be known as Viśvaksena. He will make friends (render help to Śambhu (the then Indra).

devanagari: मनुर्वै धर्मसावर्णिरेकादशम आत्मवान् । अनागतास्तत्सुताश्च सत्यधर्मादयो दश ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: manurvai dharmasāvarṇirekādaśama ātmavān anāgatāstatsutāśca satyadharmādayo daśa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: The self-controlled Dharmasāvarṇi will be the eleventh Manu who will have ten sons as Satyadharma and others.

devanagari: विहङ्गमाः कामगमा निर्वाणरुचयः सुराः । इन्द्रश्च वैधृतस्तेषामृषयश्चारुणादयः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: vihaṅgamāḥ kāmagamā nirvāṇarucayaḥ surāḥ indraśca vaidhṛtasteṣāmṛṣayaścāruṇādayaḥ
translation: The (categories of) gods will be Vihaṅgamas, Kāmagamas and Nirvāṇarucis. Their ruler Indra will be Vaidhṛta, while Aruṇa and others[6] will be the (seven) sages.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā quotes from the Harivaṃsa [Harivaṃśa?] the following sages implied in ādi: Haviṣmān, Vapuṣmān, Aruṇa, Anagha, Urudhi, Niścara and Agnitejas.

devanagari: आर्यकस्य सुतस्तत्र धर्मसेतुरिति स्मृतः । वैधृतायां हरेरंशस्त्रिलोकीं धारयिष्यति ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: āryakasya sutastatra dharmaseturiti smṛtaḥ vaidhṛtāyāṃ hareraṃśastrilokīṃ dhārayiṣyati
translation: The ray (part) of Hari known as Dharmasetu, the son of Āryaka through Vaidhṛta, will sustain and protect the three worlds.

devanagari: भविता रुद्र सावर्णी राजन्द्वादशमो मनुः । देववानुपदेवश्च देवश्रेष्ठादयः सुताः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavitā rudra sāvarṇī rājandvādaśamo manuḥ devavānupadevaśca devaśreṣṭhādayaḥ sutāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Oh King, the twelfth Manu will be Rudrasāvarṇi and his sons will be Devavān, Upadeva, Devaśreṣṭha and others.

devanagari: ऋतधामा च तत्रेन्द्रो देवाश्च हरितादयः । ऋषयश्च तपोमूर्तिस्तपस्व्याग्नीध्रकादयः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛtadhāmā ca tatrendro devāśca haritādayaḥ ṛṣayaśca tapomūrtistapasvyāgnīdhrakādayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Then Ṛtadhāman will be the Indra and the (groups of) gods will be Harita and others.[7] And the (seven) sages will be Tapomūrti, Tapasvin, Āgnīdhraka and others.[8]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.33 enumerates Harita, Rohita, Sumanas, Sukarman and Surāpa as the five groups (gaṇas) of gods.

Footnote [8]:
The list in Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.34 includes Tapasvin, Sutapas, Tapomūrti, Taporati, Tapodhṛti, Tapodyuti and Tapodhana.

devanagari: स्वधामाख्यो हरेरंशः साधयिष्यति तन्मनोः । अन्तरं सत्यसहसः सुनृतायाः सुतो विभुः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: svadhāmākhyo hareraṃśaḥ sādhayiṣyati tanmanoḥ antaraṃ satyasahasaḥ sunṛtāyāḥ suto vibhuḥ
translation: A part of Hari, by name Svadhāman, the mighty son of Satyasahas and Sūnṛtā will protect this period of Manu.

devanagari: मनुस्त्रयोदशो भाव्यो देवसावर्णिरात्मवान् । चित्रसेनविचित्राद्या देवसावर्णिदेहजाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: manustrayodaśo bhāvyo devasāvarṇirātmavān citrasenavicitrādyā devasāvarṇidehajāḥ
translation: The thirteenth Manu will be the self-controlled Devasāvarṇi and the sons of Devasāvarṇi are Citrasena, Vicitra and others.

devanagari: देवाः सुकर्मसुत्राम संज्ञा इन्द्रो दिवस्पतिः । निर्मोकतत्त्वदर्शाद्या भविष्यन्त्यृषयस्तदा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: devāḥ sukarmasutrāma saṃjñā indro divaspatiḥ nirmokatattvadarśādyā bhaviṣyantyṛṣayastadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: The (classes of) gods will be designated as Sukarma and Sutrāma[9] and Divaspati. Then Nirmoka, Tattvadarśin and others will be the (seven sages[10]).
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.37-39 adds Sudharman as the third class and each class consists of 33 gods.

Footnote [10]:
The list in Viṣṇu Purāṇa (above) differs. It consists of Nirmoha, Tattvadarśin, Niṣprakampya, Nirutsuka, Dhṛtimān. Avyaya and Sutapas.

devanagari: देवहोत्रस्य तनय उपहर्ता दिवस्पतेः । योगेश्वरो हरेरंशो बृहत्यां सम्भविष्यति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: devahotrasya tanaya upahartā divaspateḥ yogeśvaro hareraṃśo bṛhatyāṃ sambhaviṣyati
translation: The ray of Lord Hari, the master of yoga, will be born as the son of Devahotra through Bṛhatī and will procure the kingdom of three worlds to the Indra, Divaspati.

devanagari: मनुर्वा इन्द्र सावर्णिश्चतुर्दशम एष्यति । उरुगम्भीरबुधाद्या इन्द्र सावर्णिवीर्यजाः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: manurvā indra sāvarṇiścaturdaśama eṣyati urugambhīrabudhādyā indra sāvarṇivīryajāḥ
translation: They say that Indrasāvarṇi will come as the fourteenth Manu, and the sons born of him will be Uru, Gambhīrabuddhi and others.

devanagari: पवित्राश्चाक्षुषा देवाः शुचिरिन्द्रो भविष्यति । अग्निर्बाहुः शुचिः शुद्धो मागधाद्यास्तपस्विनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: pavitrāścākṣuṣā devāḥ śucirindro bhaviṣyati agnirbāhuḥ śuciḥ śuddho māgadhādyāstapasvinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

translation: The (categories of) gods will be Pavitra, Cākṣuṣa and others[11], while Śuci will be the Indra. Agnibāhu, Śuci, Śuddha, Māgadha and others will be the (seven sages).[12]
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa adds Kaniṣṭha, Bhrājata and Vācāvṛddha as three categories, (i.e. in all five)—Ibid 3.2.41.

Footnote [12]:
Viṣṇu Purāṇa adds Agnīdhra, Yukta and Jita and completes the list of seven sages.—Ibid 3.2.42.

devanagari: सत्रायणस्य तनयो बृहद्भानुस्तदा हरिः । वितानायां महाराज क्रियातन्तून्वितायिता ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: satrāyaṇasya tanayo bṛhadbhānustadā hariḥ vitānāyāṃ mahārāja kriyātantūnvitāyitā
translation: Then Hari will incarnate as the son Satrāyaṇa and Vitānā, and will be called Bṛhadbhānu. He will continue the course of sacrificial rituals, Oh great King.

devanagari: राजंश्चतुर्दशैतानि त्रिकालानुगतानि ते । प्रोक्तान्येभिर्मितः कल्पो युगसाहस्रपर्ययः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: rājaṃścaturdaśaitāni trikālānugatāni te proktānyebhirmitaḥ kalpo yugasāhasraparyayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ mā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ mā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ mā class 4 verb]

translation: These fourteen Manu periods relating to the past, present and future have been described to you, Oh king. It is by these (Manu periods) that the Kalpa comprising one thousand yugas, is measured.
